zotero.abstractNote: Purpose: To introduce a framework for the automated discovery of MRI sequences using supervised learning, termed MRzero. Methods: The framework is based on a differentiable simulation of the Bloch equations, which allows for the backpropagation of errors from the image domain to the sequence parameters. This enables the optimization of sequence parameters using gradient-based methods. We demonstrate the framework by optimizing sequences for T1 and T2 mapping, as well as for fast imaging. Results: The optimized sequences show improved performance compared to standard sequences in terms of image quality and acquisition time. The framework is flexible and can be applied to a wide range of MRI applications. Conclusion: MRzero provides a powerful tool for the automated design of MRI sequences, potentially accelerating the development of new imaging techniques.
